question_title: Will the interest in “united arab emirates” change between 2026-04-14 and 2026-04-24 according to Google Trends?
summary:
The question asks whether Google Trends interest in "united arab emirates" will increase, decrease, or stay the same (±3 threshold) from April 14 to April 24, 2026. The starting value on April 14 is 21 (in the 2026-03-25 to 2026-04-24 resolution window), already well-decayed from a massive spike to 100 on April 12 caused by Trump's announcement of a US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz after failed peace talks with Iran.

Two major forces are in tension: (1) Post-spike decay strongly favors values dropping to the 11-15 baseline within days — every non-spike 10-day transition in the data was a decrease. (2) The geopolitical context is extraordinary — an active Iran-UAE military conflict, a US blockade just beginning, and critically, the two-week ceasefire expires April 21-22, just 2-3 days before the resolution date. This creates a near-certain catalyst for renewed search interest right before measurement.

The "Doesn't change" band (18-24) is relatively narrow given the high daily volatility (std ~6.5). I estimate roughly equal probability for Increases and Decreases, with Doesn't change as the least likely outcome.

factors.description: Post-spike decay pattern: values drop from spikes to baseline (11-15) within 3-5 days. Apr 12 spike already decayed from 100 to 21 by Apr 14. Without new catalysts, Apr 24 likely settles at 11-15.

Ceasefire expires April 21-22, just 2-3 days before resolution date. Peace talks already failed in Islamabad. Neither side indicated what happens after expiration — a major catalyst for renewed interest right before Apr 24.
US naval blockade of Strait of Hormuz started April 13. Ongoing military operation creating sustained daily news coverage about confrontations, oil prices, and ship seizures.
Historical 10-day changes heavily favor decreases (7/8 non-spike transitions were decreases). The overall conflict-era trend has been downward from late March peaks.
factors.conditional: If no new major event occurs near Apr 24

factors.description: Active Iran-UAE military conflict with 537 ballistic missiles, 2,256 drone attacks intercepted. One of the most significant geopolitical crises in decades — new attacks could spike interest at any time.
Distribution of non-spike daily values: roughly equal thirds below 18, in 18-24, and above 24. Any outcome is plausible for a random conflict-era day.
SerpAPI vs pytrends measurement uncertainty of ±2-3 points at starting value 21 could shift outcomes across the ±3 threshold.

reasoning:
**Forecast: Interest in "united arab emirates" (Apr 14–24, 2026)**

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|-------------|
| Increases | **38%** |
| Decreases | **37%** |
| Doesn't change | **25%** |

**Key reasoning:**

The starting value on April 14 is **21** (after an enormous spike to 100 on April 12 driven by Trump's Hormuz blockade announcement). The core tension:

- **Favoring Decreases**: Post-spike decay is very consistent — values return to 11-15 baseline within 3-5 days. Every non-spike 10-day transition in the data showed a decrease.

- **Favoring Increases**: The **ceasefire expires April 21-22**, just 2-3 days before measurement. Peace talks already failed. Combined with the ongoing US blockade and active Iranian strikes on the UAE (537 missiles, 2,256 drones intercepted), there's substantial probability of a new spike right before resolution.

- **Doesn't change** is least likely because the ±3 window (18-24) is narrow relative to the daily volatility (~6.5 std), making it harder to land precisely in that band.

[2026 Iranian strikes on the United Arab Emirates - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_the_United_Arab_Emirates)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=united%20arab%20emirates&date=2026-03-25%202026-04-24](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=united%20arab%20emirates&date=2026-03-25%202026-04-24) for 2026-04-24 is within 3 of the value at 2026-04-14. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "united arab emirates",
  "date": "2026-03-25 2026-04-24",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
